evals.prompt: i want to start testing the experiment pipeline but don't want to burn through my whole API budget. can you run a small test first?
evals.expected_output: Suggests and executes the pilot with a dry-run first, shows cost estimate, then offers to run the actual pilot.
expectations: Suggests running the pilot (20-prompt subset) rather than the full matrix
Shows the dry-run command first (propt pilot --dry-run)
Mentions cost estimation and budget checking
Explains what the pilot validates (API keys, grading, costs, noise injection)
Provides the actual execution command (propt pilot --yes or similar)

evals.prompt: the pilot finished running. what should I do next to check if the results look good before scaling up?
evals.expected_output: Post-pilot validation checklist: check completeness, grading, costs, pass rates, then compute derived metrics and make go/no-go decision.
expectations: Suggests checking pilot completeness (all 20 prompts have runs)
Suggests checking cost data and projecting full-run cost
Suggests running compute_derived to populate derived metrics
Provides a go/no-go decision framework based on pilot results

evals.prompt: i tried running propt pilot but it said 'No config found'. what do i need to do?
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses the missing config issue and walks through setup.
expectations: Identifies the issue: config file not initialized
Suggests running propt setup to create the config via config_manager
Mentions checking API keys are set as environment variables via env_manager.check_keys()
Suggests re-running the pilot after setup completes

evals.prompt: can i run the pilot with just the providers i have configured? i only set up anthropic and google so far
evals.expected_output: Explains how the pilot uses configured providers from the registry.
expectations: Explains the pilot runs against all models configured via propt setup
Mentions model_registry.target_models() determines which models are included
Suggests propt list-models to verify which providers are configured
Notes that env_manager.check_keys() validates only configured provider keys
Explains the pilot will adjust scope to configured models only
